newsSince: Material negative: Horizon Robotics shares fell sharply (~-18.5% since June 9, near 52-week lows) as the company reported widening losses and continued cash burn, with significant insider selling over the prior three months. Profitability pressures are mounting; the August 27 earnings will be a key turnaround test.

sources.label: Meyka - Horizon Robotics slides on profitability pressure

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: NEW fundamental deterioration since Jun 9 - widening losses, continued cash burn, and significant insider selling drove a ~-18.5% drop. This dents the edge-AI commercialization case the prior up-call relied on; the pullback is now signal, not just opportunity. Down.
verdicts.bull: Leading China edge-AI ADAS platform; deep pullback (-56% YTD) and physical-AI optionality if losses inflect.
verdicts.neutral: Not a pure humanoid name; valuation cheaper but no proof of profit inflection yet.
verdicts.bear: Widening losses, cash burn, and insider selling undercut the commercialization thesis; -18.5% since Jun 9.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Persistent losses, cash burn, insider selling, China-tech sentiment
